title: Proposal
text: GLP-1 receptor signaling may regulate downstream genes involved in the cAMP/PKA pathway. In this project, we analyze publicly available data to evaluate whether tissues with higher GLP-1R expression show greater downstream pathway gene activity and whether treatment is associated with increased expression of GLP-1R and its downstream signalling genes compared with untreated samples.

title: GLP-1R Expression Predicts Activation of cAMP/PKA Signaling Pathways
text: This project looks at how GLP-1 receptor (GLP1R) expression relates to downstream signaling genes, especially in the cAMP/PKA pathway. We are using a bulk RNA-seq dataset from mice that includes different genotypes, treatments (vehicle vs drug), and tissues (liver and iWAT) to compare gene expression patterns . Our approach involves cleaning and filtering the data, running PCA for quality control, and using the Limma package to perform differential expression analysis across groups . We are specifically testing whether treated samples and GLP1R-expressing samples show higher expression of downstream pathway genes. The main deliverables will include PCA plots, volcano plots, heatmaps, and a table of differentially expressed genes. These results will help us understand how GLP-1 signaling affects gene expression across tissues.
